name: A2 Gothic
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a Grotesk, originating from a letterhead design in the early 2000s. The proportions, weight range and naming convention place it firmly in the grotesque sans-serif tradition.
summary: A clean grotesque sans originating from A2-Type's own letterhead in the early 2000s, now a full family spanning eight weights and two condensed widths.
description: Grown from an in-house letterhead face, the family covers eight weights from Thin to Black across the standard, Condensed and X Condensed widths. CNN has used a customised cut of the system for its online presence. The range suits editorial and identity work where a workhorse grotesque without strong quirks is needed.
foundry_blurb: A2-Type describe A2 Gothic as based on an early Grotesk font designed for their original letterhead in the early 2000s, forming part of a wider family that includes A2 Gothic Condensed and A2 Gothic X Condensed. Part of the system was recently customised for use by CNN online.

name: A2 Gothic Condensed
classification_notes: A condensed cut of a grotesque family, characterised by narrow proportions and exaggerated counters typical of condensed gothic sans-serif styles used in headline setting.
summary: A condensed grotesque in five weights, part of the A2 Gothic family, built around narrow proportions and exaggerated counters for headline use.
description: Part of the wider A2 Gothic grotesque family, this condensed cut trades the standard width for tight proportions and prominent counter shapes that hold up well at large display sizes. Five weights run from Regular through to Heavy, offering enough range for titling hierarchies without italic variants. The X Condensed cut extends the family further for situations demanding even tighter columns.
foundry_blurb: A2-Type describe A2 Gothic Condensed as a bold, extremely condensed yet elegant iteration of the A2 Gothic grotesque family, distinguished by its narrow proportions and exaggerated counters, making it an excellent choice for headlines and titling.

name: A2 Gothic Mono
classification_notes: A monospaced variant of a grotesque sans-serif family, derived from an early grotesk design. Single weight, intended for display use at large sizes.
summary: A single-weight monospaced grotesque drawn from A2-Type's early 2000s letterhead typeface, intended primarily for large point-size display use.
description: Derived from a grotesk A2-Type originally designed for their own letterhead around 2000, this monospaced variant offers a single Thin weight as part of the broader A2 Gothic family, which also includes condensed and extra-condensed cuts. The mono format and spindly weight suit large-scale display settings and have attracted bespoke use, including a customisation for CNN Online. Coverage is limited to standard Latin characters, numerals and basic punctuation.
foundry_blurb: A2 Gothic Mono is rooted in an early grotesk A2-Type created for their original letterhead in the early 2000s. It sits within the wider A2 Gothic system alongside A2 Gothic Condensed and A2 Gothic X Condensed, and part of the family was adapted for use by CNN Online. The single Thin weight is designed to perform particularly well at large point sizes.

name: A2 Gothic X Condensed
classification_notes: A2-Type describe it as a Grotesque family; the extreme condensation and exaggerated counters are consistent with compressed grotesque display cuts.
summary: The extra-condensed cut of the A2 Gothic family, built for headlines with narrow proportions and exaggerated counters.
description: Sitting at the compressed extreme of the A2 Gothic family, this cut offers five weights from Regular to Heavy in a very tight width suited to display and titling work. The exaggerated counters offset the extreme compression, keeping letterforms legible at large sizes. Works best in editorial and campaign contexts where column width is tight but visual impact is required.
foundry_blurb: A2-Type describe A2 Gothic X Condensed as a bold, extremely condensed yet elegant iteration of their Grotesque family, distinguished by narrow proportions and exaggerated counters, making it an excellent choice for headlines and titling.

name: A2 Mazarin
classification_notes: Rooted in the Garamond tradition via Robert Girard's early 1920s Astrée for Deberny & Peignot, the design sits firmly in the old style category with the humanist proportions and moderate contrast characteristic of French Renaissance-influenced metal type.
summary: A careful digital revival and expansion of a Garamond-inspired metal typeface originally cut for Deberny & Peignot in the early 1920s, extended from a single upright-and-italic pair into a seven-weight family.
description: Originally designed by Robert Girard around 1921 to 1923 as Astrée for the French foundry Deberny & Peignot, then recut and renamed Mazarin by Stephenson Blake in 1926, the face was historically available only in a single weight with matching italic. A2-Type have restored and reimagined it as a contemporary family across seven weights from Light to Black, each with an accompanying italic. The old style proportions and French Renaissance character of the original are preserved, giving the family a depth of historical grounding unusual in contemporary revivals.
foundry_blurb: A2-Type describe A2 Mazarin as a new font with an illustrious pedigree, tracing its origins to Robert Girard's Garamond-inspired metal typeface Astrée, published by Deberny & Peignot around 1921 to 1923 and subsequently recut by Stephenson Blake under the Mazarin name in 1926. Previously available only as a single style with italic, it has now been expertly restored and reimagined as a contemporary typeface in multiple styles.

name: A2 Record Gothic
classification_notes: A revival of Ludlow's Record Gothic, itself a copy of Morris Fuller Benton's News Gothic (1908), placing it firmly in the American grotesque tradition with the informal irregularities typical of that lineage.
summary: A revival of Ludlow's Record Gothic, tracing a lineage back to Morris Fuller Benton's News Gothic, expanded and systematised into a large extended family.
description: Ludlow's original Record Gothic began in the late 1920s as a single-weight copy of News Gothic, accruing bold, wide, condensed and extra-condensed styles piecemeal over subsequent decades. A2-Type have taken that historically irregular family and unified it into a coherent system spanning seven weights with italics, alongside Mono, Condensed, X Condensed, Stencil and Outline subfamilies. The result preserves the charm of the original's hybrid character while giving it the range needed for contemporary editorial and identity work.
foundry_blurb: A2-Type describe A2 Record Gothic as a revival of Ludlow's Record Gothic, a typeface designed in the 1920s and updated in the 1950s. Ludlow's family grew in a disjointed fashion as bold, wide, condensed and extra condensed styles were added over time, and A2-Type's project attempts to unify and systematise those disparate styles. They note that Record Gothic itself originated as a copy of ATF's News Gothic (1908) by Morris Fuller Benton, making Ludlow's eventual hybrid family a charming anomaly in type history.

name: A2 Record Gothic Condensed
classification_notes: A condensed grotesque revival rooted in Ludlow's Record Gothic, itself a derivative of Morris Fuller Benton's News Gothic. The condensed width and upright structure place it firmly in the American grotesque tradition.
summary: A revival of Ludlow's Record Gothic in condensed form, systematised across seven weights from Thin to Black.
description: Drawn from Ludlow's Record Gothic, a 1920s derivative of Morris Fuller Benton's News Gothic, this condensed subfamily brings order to what A2-Type describe as a charmingly anomalous metal type family. The original Ludlow range accrued bold, wide, condensed and extra condensed styles piecemeal over decades, and this revival unifies those disparate cuts into a consistent seven-weight system. Part of a wider A2 Record Gothic superfamily that also includes Mono, X Condensed, Stencil and Outline variants.
foundry_blurb: A2-Type describe A2 Record Gothic as a revival of Ludlow's Record Gothic, originally designed in the 1920s as a copy of ATF News Gothic and updated in the 1950s. They aimed to unify and systemise the disparate styles added over the years by the Ludlow Typograph Company, noting that the resulting hybrid family produced a charming anomaly in type history.

name: A2 Record Gothic Mono
classification_notes: A monospaced extension of a grotesque revival family, derived from Ludlow's Record Gothic, itself a copy of News Gothic. The fixed-width constraint places it firmly in the mono category while retaining the grotesque character of its source material.
summary: A monospaced extension of A2-Type's Record Gothic revival, adding a fixed-width dimension to a family rooted in Ludlow's 1920s copy of Morris Fuller Benton's News Gothic.
description: Six weights from Light to Black give the family enough range for both body and display use across print, screen and app contexts. The fixed-width spacing brings a technical, utilitarian quality that sits naturally in data-heavy layouts, code environments or wherever a grotesque mono is preferred over a neutral geometric one. It belongs to a wider A2 Record Gothic collection that also includes condensed, extra condensed, stencil and outline variants.
foundry_blurb: A2-Type describe A2 Record Gothic Mono as a monospaced addition to their A2 Record Gothic collection, itself a revival of Ludlow's Record Gothic from the 1920s. Ludlow's original was a single-weight copy of American Type Founders' News Gothic (1908) by Morris Fuller Benton; A2-Type's project unifies and systemises the disparate styles added to the family over the decades, with this mono variant intended to extend its versatility across all media.

name: A2 Record Gothic Outline
classification_notes: A single-weight outline variant of A2 Record Gothic, itself a revival of Ludlow's Record Gothic grotesque. The outline treatment shifts it into display territory, suited to headlines and decorative use rather than text.
summary: An outline cut of A2-Type's revival of Ludlow's Record Gothic, available in a single Bold weight for display use.
description: Part of A2-Type's broader systematisation of Ludlow's Record Gothic, a family with roots in the late 1920s as a copy of Morris Fuller Benton's News Gothic. The Outline variant strips the form back to its contour, making it suited to large-scale headline work, packaging and graphic applications where a lighter visual texture is needed. Available in Bold only, it sits alongside standard, mono, condensed, extra condensed and stencil cuts within the wider Record Gothic family.
foundry_blurb: A2 Record Gothic is a revival of Ludlow's Record Gothic, originally designed in the 1920s and updated in the 1950s. A2-Type describe their version as an attempt to unify and systematise the disparate styles added to the family over the decades by the Ludlow Typograph Company, noting that Ludlow's Record Gothic began as a stand-alone single-weight copy of ATF's News Gothic before growing into an unusually hybrid family.

name: A2 Record Gothic Stencil
classification_notes: A stencil variant of a grotesque revival, derived from Ludlow's Record Gothic lineage which itself descends from News Gothic. The stencil treatment and single-weight offering place it firmly in the display category.
summary: A stencil cut of A2-Type's revival of Ludlow's Record Gothic, available in a single Bold weight.
description: Part of A2-Type's broader Record Gothic family, this stencil variant draws on Ludlow's Record Gothic, itself a 1920s copy of Morris Fuller Benton's News Gothic from 1908. The Stencil is offered in Bold only, making it a focused display option rather than a versatile text family. It sits within a wider system that includes mono, condensed, extra condensed and outline subfamilies, all attempting to unify the historically disparate styles Ludlow added to the family over several decades.
foundry_blurb: A2-Type describe A2 Record Gothic as a revival of Ludlow's Record Gothic, originally designed in the 1920s as a copy of American Type Founders' News Gothic by Morris Fuller Benton, and updated in the 1950s. Their aim was to unify the disparate styles added to the family over the years by the Ludlow Typograph Company into a coherent system. The Stencil is one of several subfamilies in that system.

name: A2 Record Gothic X Condensed
classification_notes: A revival of Ludlow's Record Gothic, itself a copy of ATF News Gothic, placing it firmly in the American grotesque tradition. The extra condensed width makes it a distinct cut within a broader revival family.
summary: The extra condensed cut of A2-Type's revival of Ludlow's Record Gothic, offering seven weights from Thin to Black in a tightly compressed grotesque.
description: Part of A2-Type's systematic revival of Ludlow's Record Gothic, a typeface with roots in Morris Fuller Benton's News Gothic of 1908. The X Condensed subfamily extends the family into tightly compressed territory across seven weights, suited to headlines, posters and space-constrained editorial contexts. The broader Record Gothic family is notable for its historically hybrid character, unifying disparate styles added piecemeal by the Ludlow Typograph Company over several decades.
foundry_blurb: A2-Type describe A2 Record Gothic as a revival of Ludlow's Record Gothic, originally designed in the 1920s and updated in the 1950s. Their aim was to unify the disparate styles added to the family over the years, noting that Record Gothic itself originated as a copy of ATF News Gothic by Morris Fuller Benton, and that Ludlow's unusual accumulation of styles has produced what they call a charming anomaly in type history.

name: A2 Standard Display
classification_notes: The Display subfamily sits within a broader newspaper type system; its high contrast and range from Light to Black place it in the transitional or modern serif tradition suited to large headline setting.
summary: The display serif component of a 58-font bespoke system commissioned for the Evening Standard's 2017–18 redesign, running from Light to Black with matching italics.
description: Part of a large custom type system built by A2-Type for the Evening Standard, London's free daily tabloid distributed in 850,000 copies. The Display subfamily covers six weights from Light to Black, each with a matching italic, optimised for headline and splash use at large sizes. It sits alongside dedicated text, sans, condensed, extra-condensed and front-page splash subfamilies, all drawn as an interconnected system of 58 fonts.
foundry_blurb: A2 Standard is a collection of fonts commissioned by the Evening Standard as part of their 2017–18 redesign. The bespoke system comprises a serif text, display, sans, sans condensed, extra condensed and a special splash font for front pages, totalling 58 interconnected fonts. The Evening Standard is a free daily newspaper published in 850,000 copies Monday to Friday in tabloid format across London.

name: A2 Standard Engraved
classification_notes: An engraved variant of the A2 Standard newspaper serif system, featuring inline or engraved letterforms typical of display and titling cuts derived from classical engraving traditions.
summary: An engraved display cut from the A2 Standard system, originally commissioned for the Evening Standard's 2017–18 redesign.
description: Part of the 58-font A2 Standard system created for the Evening Standard's 2017–18 redesign, this engraved variant was produced alongside serif text, display, sans and splash cuts for a cohesive newspaper type family. Available in Bold only, it carries the inline engraved treatment associated with classical titling and decorative display work. The Evening Standard, distributed free to 850,000 London readers daily, provided the context for a tightly integrated and editorially versatile font system.
foundry_blurb: A2 Standard Engraved is part of the A2 Standard collection, a bespoke font system of 58 interconnected fonts commissioned by the Evening Standard for their 2017–18 redesign. The system spans serif text, display, sans, condensed and extra condensed, and a front-page splash font, designed for the free daily tabloid distributed across London in 850,000 copies Monday to Friday.

name: A2 Standard Sans
classification_notes: A workhorse sans-serif designed for newspaper use, spanning seven weights with condensed companions, consistent with a grotesque or realist sans classification suited to high-volume editorial contexts.
summary: A sans-serif drawn as part of a bespoke 58-font system commissioned by the Evening Standard for their 2017–18 redesign.
description: Designed by A2-Type for the Evening Standard's 2017–18 redesign, this sans sits within a larger type system that also includes serif text, display and splash cuts. Seven weights run from Thin to Heavy, each with an italic, and the family extends into Condensed and Extra Condensed widths suited to the tight columns of tabloid newspaper layout. The commission context gives it a practical editorial grounding rather than a purely expressive character.
foundry_blurb: A2 describe A2 Standard Sans as part of a collection commissioned by the Evening Standard for their 2017–18 redesign. The full A2 Standard system comprises 58 interconnected fonts covering serif text, display, sans, condensed, extra condensed and a bespoke splash face for front pages. The Evening Standard is a free daily London tabloid distributed in 850,000 copies, Monday to Friday.

name: A2 Standard Sans Condensed
classification_notes: A condensed grotesque sans designed as part of a bespoke newspaper type system, with multiple width variants suited to tabloid headline and text use.
summary: A condensed grotesque sans drawn for the Evening Standard's 2017–18 redesign, part of a 58-font bespoke system spanning text, display and splash weights.
description: Designed by A2-Type as part of the A2 Standard system commissioned by the Evening Standard for their 2017–18 overhaul, the Condensed subfamily runs seven weights from Thin to Heavy and pairs with an X Condensed variant for the tightest column and headline settings. The full system covers Serif Text, Display, Sans, Sans Condensed, Sans X Condensed and a dedicated Splash face for front pages. Built for a free daily tabloid distributed across London at 850,000 copies, the proportions and weight range reflect the demands of dense, fast-read print layouts.
foundry_blurb: A2 Standard is a collection of fonts commissioned by the Evening Standard as part of their 2017–18 redesign. The bespoke font system comprises Serif Text, Display, Sans, Sans Condensed and Extra Condensed, with a special Splash font for front pages, totalling 58 interconnected fonts. The Evening Standard is a free daily newspaper published in 850,000 copies Monday to Friday in tabloid format in London.

name: A2 Standard Sans X Condensed
classification_notes: An extra-condensed sans-serif designed for newspaper use, part of a bespoke editorial type system. Its extreme width compression and range of weights place it firmly in the grotesque tradition adapted for high-density headline and display work.
summary: The extra-condensed cut of A2-Type's bespoke type system for the Evening Standard, built for tight headline setting in tabloid newspaper format.
description: Commissioned as part of the Evening Standard's 2017–18 redesign, this extra-condensed sans runs seven weights from Thin to Heavy and sits at the compressed end of a 58-font editorial system. The X Condensed cut is built for maximum headline density in a tabloid grid, where extreme width compression allows longer counts without sacrificing size. Its siblings span serif text, display, standard and condensed sans, plus a dedicated splash font for front pages.
foundry_blurb: A2-Type describe A2 Standard as a collection of fonts commissioned by the Evening Standard for their 2017–18 redesign. The bespoke system comprises serif text, display, sans, sans condensed, and extra condensed variants, plus a special splash font for front pages, totalling 58 interconnected fonts. The Evening Standard is a free daily London tabloid published in 850,000 copies Monday to Friday.

name: A2 Standard SPLASH
classification_notes: A bespoke splash weight designed for front-page newspaper headlines, optimised for maximum impact at large sizes; its role within the A2 Standard system places it firmly in the display category.
summary: The front-page weight of the A2 Standard system, designed for the Evening Standard's 2017–18 redesign and built for maximum impact at headline scale.
description: Part of a 58-font bespoke commission for the Evening Standard, A2 Standard SPLASH is the dedicated splash weight intended for front-page headlines and high-impact display use. It runs across five weights from Light to Extrabold, sitting alongside the broader A2 Standard family that covers serif text, display, sans, condensed and extra-condensed cuts. Commissioned for a free daily tabloid distributed at 850,000 copies across London, the design carries the practical demands of high-volume newsprint at its core.
foundry_blurb: A2-Type describe A2 Standard as a collection of fonts commissioned by the Evening Standard as part of their 2017–18 redesign. The bespoke system comprises serif text, display, sans, sans condensed and extra condensed cuts, plus a dedicated splash font for front pages, totalling 58 interconnected fonts. The Evening Standard is a free daily London tabloid published Monday to Friday with a print run of 850,000 copies.

name: A2 Standard Text
classification_notes: A2 Standard Text is a serif designed for newspaper body text use, with a range of weights suited to continuous reading at small sizes, consistent with transitional serif conventions for press typography.
summary: The serif text cut from A2-Type's 58-font bespoke system commissioned for the Evening Standard's 2017–18 redesign.
description: Designed as part of a large commissioned type system for the Evening Standard, this text serif sits alongside display, sans, condensed and splash variants totalling 58 interconnected fonts. Six weights from Light to Black, each with a matching italic, give editors a full range for body text across a tabloid distributed in 850,000 daily copies across London. The system was built to serve print, web, app and broadcast contexts simultaneously.
foundry_blurb: A2 Standard is a font collection commissioned by the Evening Standard for their 2017–18 redesign. The system includes a serif Text, Display, Sans, Sans Condensed, Extra Condensed and a Splash font for front pages, totalling 58 interconnected fonts designed for London's primary free daily evening newspaper.

name: A23D Outline
classification_notes: A mono-linear rounded geometric alphabet rendered as a wireframe exoskeleton, derived from a 3D-printed letterpress project. Its structural, technical character places it firmly in the display category.
summary: A wireframe exoskeleton alphabet derived from a 3D-printed letterpress commission for New North Press, available in a single Regular weight.
description: Born from a commission by New North Press in London, A23D Outline is the outward shell of A23D Solid, a mono-linear rounded skeleton designed to be extruded into a wireframe mesh and 3D printed as working letterpress type. The Outline variant exposes that mesh structure as a standalone display face, retaining the geometric, rounded character of its parent. Available in one weight, its use is naturally narrow but precise: headline work, print projects and contexts where the mechanical, gridded aesthetic is the point.
foundry_blurb: A2-Type describe A23D Solid as the starting point and hidden core of the design, created as a skeleton to be extruded into a wireframe mesh for 3D printing as a fully functional letterpress font set for New North Press. A23D Outline, released in 2018, is their perfectly crafted exoskeleton iteration of that Solid style.

name: A23D Solid
classification_notes: A mono-linear, rounded, single-weight display face designed as a structural skeleton for a 3D-printed letterpress wireframe alphabet. Its geometric, even-stroked construction and single style place it firmly in the display category.
summary: A mono-linear, rounded display face originally designed as the structural skeleton for a 3D-printed letterpress wireframe alphabet commissioned by New North Press.
description: Designed by A2-Type as the hidden core from which the A23D letterpress wireframe system was built, this single-weight face uses even strokes and rounded terminals throughout. The solid form was extruded into a wireframe mesh, then 3D printed to create a working letterpress font set for New North Press in London. A23D Outline (2018) offers a companion exoskeleton iteration built directly from the same underlying geometry.
foundry_blurb: A2-Type describe A23D Solid as the starting point and hidden core for their A23D letterpress wireframe alphabet, commissioned by New North Press in London. The mono-linear, rounded solid style was designed as a skeleton, later extruded into a wireframe mesh that was 3D printed and supplied to the client as a fully working letterpress font set. A23D Outline is described as a perfectly crafted exoskeleton iteration of the Solid style.

name: Afterall
classification_notes: Designed primarily for long-form editorial text in a journal and book series context, with subtle quirks and flourishes becoming more apparent at display sizes — characteristic of a humanist text serif.
summary: A text serif originally commissioned for the Afterall Journal and One Work book series, released publicly by A2-Type after an extensive editorial redesign.
description: Conceived as part of A2/SW/HK's redesign of the Afterall Journal and its accompanying One Work series, the family runs across five weights from Regular to Black, each with a matching italic. The design prioritises legibility at text sizes for long-form essays and features, while subtle quirks and flourishes emerge at display scales. Five weights give it enough range to handle hierarchy within a considered editorial system.
foundry_blurb: Afterall Serif is a concise custom font family originally created for the Afterall Journal (2003–) and the Afterall One Work series (2006–) as part of A2/SW/HK's extensive redesign. Conceived primarily as a text font for long-form features and essays, it is highly readable with a balanced impression on the printed page, while retaining a distinctive voice with subtle quirks and flourishes that become noticeable at larger sizes.

name: Agriculture
classification_notes: Described as soft, organic and idiosyncratic with a flowing, rhythmic appearance, placing it firmly in the humanist sans tradition rather than a geometric or grotesque model.
summary: A soft, rhythmically drawn sans serif from A2-Type with an organic, idiosyncratic character across three weights.
description: Available in Light, Medium and Bold, Agriculture has an unhurried, flowing quality that sets it apart from more neutral humanist sans serifs. Its organic construction gives it a distinctive personality suited to editorial, branding and cultural projects where a geometric or grotesque would feel too cold. The family is compact but coherent, with the weight range covering most everyday typographic needs.
foundry_blurb: A2-Type describe Agriculture as a soft, organic and idiosyncratic sans serif typeface, with its design underpinned by a flowing, rhythmic appearance.

name: Antwerp
classification_notes: Drawn from sixteenth-century sources and the Plantin-Moretus Museum archive, Antwerp sits firmly in the old-style (Garalde) tradition, with the humanist stroke contrast and bracketed serifs characteristic of that period.
summary: A text serif drawing directly on the sixteenth-century type specimens held at the Plantin-Moretus Museum in Antwerp, Belgium.
description: Five weights from Light to Bold, each with a matching italic, make this a workable family for newspapers, books and magazines. The Plantin-Moretus archive provides the historical grounding, giving the design an old-style warmth without excessive archaism. Sits comfortably in long-form editorial settings where legibility at text sizes matters more than display presence.
foundry_blurb: A2-Type describe Antwerp as an elegant, versatile text face inspired by sixteenth-century typefaces and more directly by the type specimen collection in the archive at the Plantin-Moretus Museum in Antwerp, Belgium, designed for use in today's newspapers, books and magazines.

name: Archi
classification_notes: Archi is a mono-linear typeface, meaning all strokes share an equal weight, inspired by the mechanical letterforms found in architectural drawings and routing processes. This places it within sans-serif territory, closer to a technical or engineering grotesque than a conventional humanist or geometric model.
summary: A single-weight mono-linear text face drawn from the lettering conventions of architectural drawings, originally commissioned for a 2004 Hayward Gallery exhibition.
description: Designed for the 'Eyes, Lies and Illusions' exhibition at the Hayward Gallery in London, Archi takes its cue from the mechanical routing process and the uniform-stroke lettering typical of technical and architectural draughtsmanship. The result is a single Regular weight with even stroke widths throughout, lending it a clinical yet readable quality across point sizes. Its narrow application range makes it best suited to contexts where a technical or drafting aesthetic is intentional.
foundry_blurb: A2-Type describe Archi as a mono-linear text face originally created for the Hayward Gallery exhibition 'Eyes, Lies and Illusions' in 2004, inspired by the mono-linear typography found in architectural drawings and the mechanical process of routing, and note that it is superbly legible at all point sizes.

name: Avaunt Stencil
classification_notes: A stencil typeface with serif letterforms, designed for magazine display use. The stencil cuts give it a functional-industrial character while the underlying serif structure keeps it elegant.
summary: A stencil serif commissioned by art director Matt Willey for AVAUNT Magazine in 2014, later expanded into five weights with broader language support.
description: Originally drawn in a single weight with a limited character set for Matt Willey's design of AVAUNT Magazine, the family was reworked in 2016 with five weights running from Light to Bold. The serif letterforms give the stencil construction an elegance that suits editorial display work rather than purely industrial or military contexts. Language support was expanded alongside the weight range, making it more viable beyond its original commission.
foundry_blurb: Avaunt Stencil is described by A2-Type as an elegant serif stencil font, originally commissioned by art director Matt Willey (now at Pentagram) for AVAUNT Magazine in early 2014. Initially released in one weight with a limited character set, it was reworked in 2016 into multiple weights with an expanded glyph set covering multiple languages.

name: Aveny-T
classification_notes: Inspired by early twentieth-century modernist and avant-garde lettering, Aveny-T sits within the geometric sans tradition, originally conceived as an all-caps display face before being extended with lowercase for retail release.
summary: Originally a capitals-only poster face designed for a Danish theatre in 2000, now expanded into an eleven-weight family with full lowercase.
description: Drawn from early twentieth-century modernist and avant-garde lettering, the face was first created as a bespoke capitals-only typeface for the Danish theatre Aveny-T. The updated retail release adds lowercase glyphs and a broad weight range running from Ultralight to Ultra, making it usable across both headline and identity contexts. No italics are included, keeping the family firmly in the upright, architectural tradition of its source material.
foundry_blurb: A2-Type describe Aveny-T as inspired by modernist-era and avant-garde lettering from the early twentieth century, originally designed as a poster typeface in capital letters only for the Danish theatre Aveny-T in 2000. The updated family adds a full character set with lowercase and glyphs for modern use across numerous weights.

name: Beckett
classification_notes: Rooted in machine-cut wood type traditions, Beckett is a sans-serif with hard, unmodulated edges characteristic of industrial grotesques derived from nineteenth-century wood type. The absence of italics and rounded variants suggests a display-leaning grotesque lineage.
summary: A wood-type-derived grotesque originally cut for the Faber & Faber 'Samuel Beckett: Complete Works' series in 2009, expanded to 11 weights in 2017.
description: Originally commissioned for a specific Faber & Faber publishing project, the design draws on machine-cut wood type, giving it a hard, unmodulated edge suited to cover work and display settings. The family spans 11 weights from Extralight to Heavy, with a companion Beckett Round subfamily. There are no italics, keeping the family focused on upright display and editorial use.
foundry_blurb: A2-Type describe Beckett as originally designed for the Faber & Faber 'Samuel Beckett: Complete Works' book cover series in 2009, with its character rooted in machine-cut wood type and its hard edges giving it an immediate presence on any surface. The family was updated and re-released in October 2017 with 11 styles ranging from Extralight to Heavy.

name: Beckett Round
classification_notes: Derived from a machine-cut wood type revival, Beckett Round is a display-weight sans with softened, rounded terminals throughout, placing it firmly in the rounded sans subcategory of display type.
summary: A rounded variant of A2-Type's wood-type-inspired Beckett, sharing identical proportions so the two can be swapped without affecting text flow.
description: Beckett Round takes the machine-cut wood type source material of the original Beckett and applies rounded corners across the entire letterform set, producing a softer character while retaining the same weight system and proportions. Eleven weights run from Extralight to Heavy, with no italics in the family. Because metrics are matched to the parent Beckett, the two faces can be used interchangeably within a layout without reflowing text.
foundry_blurb: A2-Type describe Beckett Round as a version of their Beckett font with rounded corners, transforming the original machine-cut wood type inspired design into a softer, less hard-edged counterpart. The proportions and weight system match Beckett exactly, allowing the two fonts to be interchanged without affecting text flow or line endings.

name: Bermondsey Sans
classification_notes: Described as mechanical and inspired by stone-carved lettering, Bermondsey Sans has a constructed, engineered quality that places it squarely in the geometric sans tradition, with uppercase letterforms driving the overall character.
summary: A mechanically constructed sans inspired by stone-carved lettering on a South London building, with lowercase and numerals invented to complement the distinctive uppercase source material.
description: Drawn from the carved lettering on St. Saviour's House in Bermondsey, the uppercase carries the engineered simplicity of its architectural source. The lowercase letters, numerals and punctuation are A2-Type's own invention, built from details observed in the capitals rather than borrowed from any typographic tradition. Available in five weights from Regular to Black, it suits display and identity work where a slightly idiosyncratic, place-specific character is wanted.
foundry_blurb: A2-Type describe Bermondsey Sans as a somewhat mechanical alphabet inspired by stone-carved letters on the façade of St. Saviour's House in South London. The original letters are simply engineered but display unique character and subtle charm. The lowercase letters, numerals and other characters are invented by A2-Type and based on key details found in the uppercase.

name: Biilmann
classification_notes: A digitised revival of a 1932 geometric alphabet by Danish architect and graphic designer Gunnar Biilmann Petersen, the typeface sits within the modernist Scandinavian sans-serif tradition with clean, constructed letterforms.
summary: A digital revival of a 1932 alphabet by Danish architect Gunnar Biilmann Petersen, redrawn by A2-Type from archive originals held at the Danish Design Museum.
description: Working directly from Gunnar Biilmann Petersen's original drawings held in the Danish Design Museum's archive, A2-Type secured permission from the Biilmann family in 2018 to restore and reimagine the alphabet as a contemporary digital typeface. The family spans seven weights from Thin to Black, with no italics, keeping the upright, constructed character of the source material. It sits firmly within the modernist Scandinavian sans-serif tradition and suits identity and editorial work that calls for historical grounding without period pastiche.
foundry_blurb: A2-Type describe Biilmann as a modern interpretation of an original alphabet drawn in 1932 by renowned Danish architect and graphic designer Gunnar Biilmann Petersen. Working directly from GBP's original drawings held in the Danish Design Museum's archive, A2-Type was granted special permission by the Biilmann family in 2018 to restore and reimagine the alphabet as a contemporary digital sans serif rooted in Danish typeface tradition.

name: Boing
classification_notes: A rounded sans-serif with softened terminals throughout, positioned as both a display and text face. The geometric softness and even stroke weight place it firmly in the rounded sans subcategory.
summary: A rounded sans-serif from A2-Type spanning six weights with italics, designed to work across display and text settings.
description: Soft terminals and even stroke weights give it a friendly, approachable tone without sacrificing legibility at smaller sizes. The family runs from Thin to Bold with matching italics across all weights, and a monospaced variant, Boing Mono, has since been added to the library. The range makes it serviceable for branding, editorial and screen work where warmth is wanted without going fully playful.
foundry_blurb: A2-Type describe Boing as a bold, rounded sans-serif designed for a range of communication needs, characterising it as soft yet solid and imposing yet friendly. Conceived primarily as a display face, they note it is highly readable and works equally well as a text face, with Boing Mono added as a companion to the library.

name: Boing Mono
classification_notes: A monospaced companion to the Boing family, sharing its geometric, rounded character while enforcing fixed-width spacing across all six weights.
summary: A fixed-width companion to A2-Type's Boing family, offered in six upright weights with a rounded, geometric character.
description: Designed as the default monospaced counterpart to the Boing family, it retains the geometric warmth of the parent while applying fixed-width spacing throughout. Six weights run from Thin to Bold, all upright with no italic cuts. Suited to coding interfaces, tabular work or any setting where the Boing aesthetic needs monospaced discipline.
foundry_blurb: A2-Type describe Boing Mono as the long-awaited default companion to their Boing font family, designed with six matching upright font weights.

name: Condensed Sans No.10
classification_notes: A condensed grotesque sans-serif revival drawn from Stephenson Blake & Co.'s Victorian-era metal type, retaining the upright, tight proportions characteristic of nineteenth-century condensed grotesques.
summary: A revival of Stephenson Blake & Co.'s Condensed Sans Serif No.10, drawn directly from the Sheffield foundry's 14-point metal type cut.
description: The design takes Stephenson Blake & Co.'s nineteenth-century Condensed Sans Serif No.10 as its source, with A2-Type describing the approach as free-spirited rather than strictly faithful. Five weights run from Regular to Black, all maintaining the tight, upright proportions that defined the original metal cut. Without italics, it is oriented towards display and headline work where condensed grotesque letterforms are needed with period character.
foundry_blurb: A2-Type describe Condensed Sans No.10 as a free-spirited revival of Stephenson Blake & Co.'s Condensed Sans Serif No.10, directly inspired by the cut made for their 14-point metal type. The family is available in five weights: Regular, Medium, Bold, ExtraBold and Black.

name: Copenhagen
classification_notes: A revival of a vernacular municipal signage sans, fitting squarely within the grotesque tradition with an industrial, no-nonsense character typical of mid-century European public lettering.
summary: A revival of the sans serif lettering found on municipal signs across Copenhagen, retaining its utilitarian, industrial character.
description: Drawn from the vernacular sans serif found on Copenhagen's citywide municipal signage, this three-weight family preserves the no-nonsense quality of its source material. The letterforms carry the slight idiosyncrasies of public lettering rather than the polish of a constructed geometric or humanist grotesque. It suits applications where an unpretentious, quietly distinctive sans is needed.
foundry_blurb: A2-Type describe Copenhagen as a revival of a vernacular sans serif typeface found on municipal signs used citywide in Copenhagen, calling it a no-nonsense industrial font with a unique charm.

name: CPH Signs
classification_notes: Revived from hand-painted Copenhagen street sign lettering, the letterforms carry a humanist warmth and calligraphic origin typical of mid-century European signage traditions, placing it firmly in the humanist sans category.
summary: A revival of Copenhagen's hand-painted enamel street sign lettering, adapted into a five-weight family with understated, calligraphic warmth.
description: Drawn from the white-on-dark-blue enamel signs still visible across Copenhagen, CPH Signs translates a civic lettering tradition into a usable typeface across five weights from Light to Bold. Its calligraphic origins give it a quiet elegance that has attracted both brand and editorial use, including SuperBrugsen's corporate identity and headline setting in the Danish newspaper Berlingske. The absence of italics keeps it focused, best suited to signage, packaging and display work where its heritage reads clearly.
foundry_blurb: A2-Type describe CPH Signs as a free-spirited revival of Copenhagen's street sign lettering, traditionally painted in white on dark blue enamel and still visible throughout the city today. They note its adoption by Danish supermarket chain SuperBrugsen as a corporate typeface across signage, packaging and price tags, and its use as a headline face in the Danish newspaper Berlingske.

name: CPH Tram
classification_notes: A revival of early 20th-century Danish tram signage lettering, the hand-painted origins give it a humanist grotesque quality rather than a mechanically precise construction.
summary: A seven-weight revival of the hand-painted destination lettering used on Copenhagen trams around 1920.
description: Drawn from original hand-painted tram destination signs dating to around 1920, the family carries the irregular warmth of signpainting into a usable digital grotesque. Seven weights run from Light to Black with no italics, keeping the range focused and suited to display and wayfinding contexts. The source material gives it a character distinct from constructed grotesques of the same era.
foundry_blurb: A2-Type describe CPH Tram as a revival of the typeface used on destination signs displayed on the front of Danish trams circa 1920, with original hand-painted signs as the starting point, lovingly revived as a contemporary design now available in seven unique weights.

name: CPH Tram Sans
classification_notes: A clean, contemporary sans described as modern and streamlined, positioned as a 2020s counterpart to the old-style CPH Tram; lacks the ornamental qualities of its predecessor, pointing to a geometric or neo-grotesque construction.
summary: A modernised, seven-weight sans cut derived from A2-Type's original CPH Tram, trading old-style character for a cleaner, contemporary feel.
description: Where the original CPH Tram drew on 1920s lettering conventions, CPH Tram Sans strips that ornamentation away in favour of a leaner, more contemporary form suited to current design work. Seven weights run from Light to Black with no italics, covering the range expected of a workhorse display and text sans. The family suits branding, editorial and wayfinding contexts where a clean, upright sans is preferable to anything with historical warmth.
foundry_blurb: A2-Type describe CPH Tram Sans as a sans serif cut of their CPH Tram font, offering a more streamlined and less flamboyant design than the old-style original. They position it as modern, clean and contemporary — less 1920s, more 2020s — available in seven unique weights.

name: CWM
classification_notes: CWM draws on Cold War era poster typography and printed ephemera, resulting in a geometric sans with the restrained, rational character typical of mid-century European modernism.
summary: Originally designed in 2008 as the principal typeface for the V&A Museum's 'Cold War Modern' exhibition catalogue, drawing on mid-century poster and ephemera typography.
description: Commissioned for the V&A's 'Cold War Modern: Design 1945–1970' publication, the typeface takes its cues from the graphic language of that era: restrained geometric forms with a modernist, institutional character. It has since been expanded from its original weight to a family of six, running from Thin to Bold. Works well in catalogue and editorial contexts where a controlled, period-informed sans is needed.
foundry_blurb: A2-Type describe CWM as drawn from typography, poster design and printed ephemera of the Cold War era, originally created in 2008 as the principal typeface for the V&A Museum's 'Cold War Modern: Design 1945–1970' catalogue. The family has since been updated with two additional contrasting weights.

name: Dagspris
classification_notes: A single-style revival of a handcrafted price sign lettering, giving it a utilitarian, vernacular display character rather than the refinement of a text face.
summary: A single-style revival of a handcrafted Danish price-sign lettering originally created by architect and designer Gunnar Biilmann Petersen for Munke Mølle.
description: Based on a handmade price sign by Danish architect and designer Gunnar Biilmann Petersen (1897-1968), the typeface carries the directness and slight irregularity of its retail signage origins. A2-Type have revived it as a single Regular weight intended for everyday use rather than purely archival display. Its utilitarian source material gives it an unpretentious quality suited to packaging, editorial and branding projects seeking a vernacular Scandinavian flavour.
foundry_blurb: A2-Type describe Dagspris as a utilitarian single-style typeface inspired by a handcrafted price sign made by Danish architect and designer Gunnar Biilmann Petersen (1897-1968) for Munke Mølle, revived with care and designed for everyday use.

name: Dane
classification_notes: A contemporary sans-serif drawn from Danish industrial sign lettering of the 1940s, with wide proportions and humanist detailing such as the double-storey 'g' placing it within the grotesque tradition.
summary: A sans-serif rooted in Danish industrial sign lettering of the 1940s, with wide proportions and a double-storey 'g' across six weights.
description: Originally designed in 2002 and expanded through several rounds of development up to 2017, Dane draws on the utilitarian lettering found on Danish industrial signage from the mid-twentieth century. Wide, generous proportions give it a confident presence at display sizes, while the double-storey lowercase 'g' adds a humanist note that distinguishes it from more neutral grotesques. Six weights from Light to Extrabold make it workable for branding and identity projects where some typographic character is wanted without heavy stylisation.
foundry_blurb: A2-Type describe Dane as a contemporary sans-serif typeface inspired by Danish industrial sign lettering from circa the 1940s. It features wide, generous proportions and a distinctive lowercase double-storey 'g' to add personality across all six weights. First designed in 2002, it was expanded in 2008 and updated with additional weights in 2012 and 2017.

name: Danmark
classification_notes: Danmark is explicitly described as a stencil cut serif, combining serif letterform structure with stencil interruptions in the strokes, drawing on architectural modernist references rather than historical type revivals.
summary: A stencil serif drawn from the architectural details of the Louisiana Museum of Modern Art in Denmark, available in five weights.
description: Inspired by the discreet modernism and architectural language of the Louisiana Museum of Modern Art, Danmark translates structural stencil cuts into a serif typeface across five weights from Thin to Bold. The reference point is specific enough to give the design a coherent visual logic rather than generic stencil pastiche. It suits editorial, cultural and institutional work where a restrained, architecturally informed character is appropriate.
foundry_blurb: A2-Type describe Danmark as an original stencil cut serif font inspired by the unique architectural details and discreet modernism of the buildings at the Louisiana Museum of Modern Art in Denmark.

name: English 1766
classification_notes: Rooted in the Caslon tradition, which places it firmly in the old style serif category. A2-Type describe it as a contemporary interpretation rather than a strict revival, giving it a broader weight range than historical Caslon cuts.
summary: A contemporary interpretation of Caslon in 20 styles, drawing on historical type specimen research rather than direct revival.
description: Built from close study of historical Caslon foundry type specimens and more recent digital revivals, English 1766 takes the quintessentially British old style as a starting point rather than a template. Ten weights from Thin to Heavy, each with a matching italic, extend the family well beyond the range of any original Caslon cutting. The breadth of the weight range makes it serviceable across editorial, branding and text work where an old style serif with contemporary flexibility is needed.
foundry_blurb: A2-Type describe English 1766 as a new font in 20 styles inspired by Caslon, developed after intensive research into historical type specimen pages printed with Caslon foundry type across multiple sizes and conditions, as well as study of recent digital revivals. They are clear that it is not a historical revival but a contemporary interpretation of the classic design.

name: Ergonomics
classification_notes: A single-style headline and signing face with arrow terminals, designed for exhibition signage and display purposes, placing it firmly in the display category with geometric sans characteristics.
summary: A single-style display and signing face created for the Design Museum London's 2009 Ergonomics exhibition, distinguished by its arrow terminals.
description: Originally commissioned as a display and wayfinding typeface for the Design Museum London's 'Ergonomics – Real Design' exhibition in 2009–10, the family comprises three variants: the standard cut, a Closed version, and an Arrows version that foregrounds the distinctive directional terminals. The design is uppercase-only and purpose-built for large-scale headline use. Its exhibition origins give it a functional, institutional character that separates it from purely decorative display faces.
foundry_blurb: A2-Type describe Ergonomics as a single-style headline font designed as a display and signing face for the 'Ergonomics – Real Design' exhibition at the Design Museum in London, 2009–10, featuring distinctive arrow terminals.

name: Estd.
classification_notes: Described by A2-Type as 'heavy duty mono-linear', the letterforms derive from inscribed tile work rather than typographic tradition, producing a geometric, even-stroked sans with an archaic, engraved quality.
summary: A mono-linear sans drawn from inscribed floor tiles in Basque Country churches, digitised across seven weights with a broad character set.
description: The letterforms are rooted in the even-stroked inscriptions found on church floor tiles in the French Basque Country, giving the family a quiet, archaic rigidity that sets it apart from conventional geometric sans designs. Seven weights run from Thin to Bold without italics, keeping the family focused on display and titling applications where the inscriptional quality reads clearly. The extended character set supports multiple languages, broadening its practical reach beyond its niche historical source material.
foundry_blurb: A2-Type describe Estd. as inspired by 'heavy duty mono-linear' inscriptions found on floor tiles in French Basque Country churches. Built from multiple historical sources and digitally reimagined, the typeface features an advanced character set supporting multiple languages across numerous font weights.

name: Eyes Lies
classification_notes: A single-weight all-caps display face drawn from Op Art and pre-cinematic optical devices, with no text weight or italic — purely a headline and display typeface.
summary: A single-weight all-caps display face created for the Hayward Gallery's 2004 'Eye, Lies and Illusions' exhibition, drawing on Op Art and pre-cinematic optical devices.
description: Designed in 2004 alongside A2-Type's Archi typeface for the Hayward Gallery show, it takes its visual cues from optical toys and devices shown in the exhibition as well as the Op Art movement. The result is a headline-only face, uppercase throughout, suited to short bursts of display text where visual tension is wanted. A single Regular weight is available with no italic or condensed variants.
foundry_blurb: A2-Type describe Eyes Lies as an experimental headline font made for the Hayward Gallery's 'Eye, Lies and Illusions' exhibition in 2004, with inspiration drawn from pre-cinematic optical devices featured in the show and the Op Art movement. It was designed alongside their Archi typeface, which handled text panels in the same exhibition.

name: Flavin
classification_notes: A rounded sans-serif with terminals that echo the cylindrical form of a fluorescent tube, originally drawn for exhibition signage and text use.
summary: A rounded sans-serif drawn for the Dan Flavin retrospective at the Hayward Gallery in 2006, its forms referencing the shape of a fluorescent tube.
description: Originally commissioned as a signage and text font for 'Dan Flavin: A Retrospective' at the Hayward Gallery, the design carries a quiet conceptual logic in its rounded terminals, which echo the cylindrical profile of Flavin's fluorescent light works. Five weights run from Light to Bold without italics, making it a compact family suited to cultural and editorial contexts. The well-balanced proportions hold up at both display and text sizes.
foundry_blurb: A2-Type describe Flavin as a rounded font with a well-balanced form that echoes the shape of a fluorescent tube light, originally drawn as a signage and text font for the 2006 Dan Flavin retrospective at the Hayward Gallery.

name: FM
classification_notes: FM is explicitly described as a slab serif with 7 weights and italics. Its combination of authority and elegance across headline and text sizes places it in the contemporary slab serif tradition.
summary: A slab serif family in seven weights with italics, designed to work across headlines, pull quotes and body text.
description: Spanning Thin to Black with a matching italic for each weight, this slab serif covers the full range of editorial roles without switching families. The design balances authority at large sizes with readability in continuous text, making it a single-family solution for print and digital publications. A2-Type position it as equally at home in captions as in display settings.
foundry_blurb: A2-Type describe FM as a versatile slab serif that exudes elegance and authority, combining modernity with timelessness. The family of 7 weights with corresponding italics is positioned as an ideal editorial choice, setting equally well for headlines and pull quotes as it does for body copy and captions.

name: Foundation Sans 44
classification_notes: Part of A2-Type's Foundation revival suite drawing on historical specimens from America, France and Nordic countries; the upright, structured letterforms and broad weight range place it firmly in the grotesque sans tradition.
summary: A ten-weight grotesque sans drawn from historical American, French and Nordic typeface specimens, forming part of A2-Type's 100-font Foundation collection.
description: Foundation Sans 44 spans ten weights from Thin to Heavy, each with a matching italic, giving it considerable range for both text and display applications. It sits alongside Foundation Sans Wide and Foundation Sans Condensed as width variants within the broader Foundation suite, which also includes serif and Didot sub-families. The revival basis draws on specimens from America, France and Nordic countries, lending the family a grounded, historically informed character without leaning into pastiche.
foundry_blurb: Foundation is described by A2-Type as a suite of revival fonts inspired by historical typeface specimens originating from America, France and Nordic countries. The Foundation collection now consists of 100 fonts across sans, wide, condensed and serif sub-families.

name: Foundation Sans Condensed
classification_notes: Part of the Foundation suite of revival fonts drawn from historical American, French and Nordic specimens; the condensed cut follows the grotesque tradition of narrow, upright sans-serifs suited to tight settings and display work.
summary: The condensed subfamily of A2-Type's Foundation collection, drawing on historical grotesque specimens from America, France and Nordic countries across ten weights with matching italics.
description: Twenty styles span Thin to Heavy, each with a companion italic, giving designers a wide tonal range in a tight horizontal footprint. Foundation Sans Condensed sits within a broader 100-font suite that includes upright and wide sans cuts alongside serif and Didot variants. The revival brief, rooted in historical specimens from three distinct typographic traditions, gives the family more character than a purely contemporary condensed grotesque would carry.
foundry_blurb: Foundation Sans Condensed is part of A2-Type's Foundation collection, a suite of revival fonts inspired by historical typeface specimens from America, France and Nordic countries. The full collection now spans 100 fonts across sans, wide, condensed and serif subfamilies.

name: Foundation Sans Wide
classification_notes: Part of A2-Type's Foundation collection, a revival suite drawn from historical American, French and Nordic specimens; the Wide variant extends the base grotesque into expanded proportions across a large weight range.
summary: The wide-width member of A2-Type's Foundation collection, a revival grotesque drawn from American, French and Nordic historical specimens across ten weights with matching italics.
description: Foundation Sans Wide spans ten weights from Thin to Heavy, each with a matching italic, giving a total of 20 styles in this width variant alone. It sits within the broader Foundation suite of 100 fonts, which also includes the standard and condensed sans cuts alongside serif and Didot serif families. The expanded proportions suit display and branding work where a confident, wide-set grotesque is needed.
foundry_blurb: Foundation is a suite of revival fonts inspired by historical typeface specimens from America, France and Nordic countries. The collection now comprises 100 fonts across sans, condensed and serif families.

name: Foundation Serif
classification_notes: Described as a revival inspired by historical typeface specimens from America, France and Nordic countries, placing it firmly in the serif revival tradition with a broad, multi-source historical reference.
summary: A 20-style revival serif drawing on historical specimens from America, France and the Nordic countries, spanning Thin to Heavy with matching italics.
description: Part of A2-Type's wider Foundation collection of 100 fonts, this serif member of the suite takes its cues from historical typeface specimens across three distinct geographic traditions. The weight range runs from Thin through to Heavy, with italic companions at every step, giving it flexibility across editorial, print and branding contexts. The collection also includes Foundation Serif Didot as a separate sub-family, extending the historical reach further.
foundry_blurb: A2-Type describe Foundation as a suite of revival fonts inspired by historical typeface specimens originating from America, France and Nordic countries, with the full Foundation collection now comprising 100 fonts.

name: Foundation Serif Didot
classification_notes: The name directly references Didot, the canonical French neoclassical serif tradition characterised by extreme stroke contrast, hairline serifs and vertical axis — placing this firmly in the didone subcategory.
summary: A Didot-inspired serif revival from A2-Type's Foundation collection, spanning ten weights with matching italics for a total of twenty styles.
description: Part of A2-Type's broader Foundation suite of revival typefaces drawn from historical American, French and Nordic specimens, this family takes the French Didot tradition as its source — bringing sharp stroke contrast and hairline serifs to a contemporary weight range. Ten weights run from Thin to Heavy, each paired with an italic, giving designers a wide tonal range within a single neoclassical voice. Suited to fashion, luxury editorial and high-contrast display work where the Didot idiom is called for.
foundry_blurb: Foundation is described by A2-Type as a suite of revival fonts inspired by historical typeface specimens from America, France and Nordic countries, with the full Foundation collection now comprising 100 fonts.

name: Game Over
classification_notes: A pixel font drawn in the style of 8-bit video game typography, designed for low-resolution screens and display use. Its single weight and grid-based construction place it firmly in the display category.
summary: A single-weight pixel font modelled on typography from the golden age of 8-bit video games, optimised for low-resolution display use.
description: A pixel font drawn from the visual vocabulary of 8-bit arcade and home console games, offered in a single Regular weight. The design is built for low-resolution screens, though its retro character makes it a natural fit for gaming-adjacent branding, titles and editorial contexts. Only uppercase, lowercase and basic punctuation are included, reflecting the constrained character sets typical of its source era.
foundry_blurb: A2-Type describe Game Over as a playful pixel font inspired by typography from the golden age of 8-bit video games, originally designed for screens and optimised for use in low-resolution displays.

name: GB.
classification_notes: A bold sans serif drawing on letterpress wood type and early sans serif specimens, placing it firmly in the grotesque tradition with historical vernacular roots.
summary: A sans serif family originating from just two letters found in a Morgan Press Type catalogue, extended into a seven-weight grotesque with wood type and early specimen influences.
description: The design began with a single page from the Morgan Press Type catalogue bearing only a capital G and a capital B, which A2-Type used as the seed for a full grotesque family. The resulting seven weights run from Light to Black, balancing the directness of letterpress wood alphabets with the cohesion expected of a contemporary type system. The family has received a Tokyo TDC Prize and an ISTD Certificate of Typographic Excellence.
foundry_blurb: A2-Type describe GB. as a bold sans serif inspired by two capital letters found in the Morgan Press Type catalogue. Starting from just G and B, the challenge was to build a harmonious and coherent design, drawing further on letterpress wood alphabets and early sans serif fonts from historical type specimens. They call it a striking balance of tradition and modernity.

name: Glyptotek
classification_notes: Derived from archival lettering on a Danish Neoclassical museum building, the family is unusual in combining serif uppercase with sans-serif lowercase as its primary source material, making it a display revival rather than a conventional text family.
summary: A revival drawn from the original lettering of the Ny Carlsberg Glyptotek in Copenhagen, preserving its peculiar mix of serif uppercase and sans-serif lowercase as a distinct subfamily.
description: Based on lettering found on Vilhelm Dahlerup's 1880s museum building in central Copenhagen, the family offers four cuts: a pure sans (A), a matching serif (B), and two mixed-case variants (C and D) that replicate the original alphabet's unusual pairing of serif caps with sans lowercase. All four subfamilies are available at Regular weight only, and the full family is provided free of charge to designers working directly with the Ny Carlsberg Glyptotek on signage, publications or campaigns. Its specificity of origin gives it a credibility that generic historicist typefaces lack.
foundry_blurb: A2-Type describe Glyptotek as a contemporary restoration and revival inspired by the original lettering on the Ny Carlsberg Glyptotek museum in Copenhagen, designed by Vilhelm Dahlerup in the early 1880s. They note that the source alphabet is a peculiar mixed design combining serif uppercase with sans-serif lowercase, and that the new family is organised into three categories: a sans (A), a serif (B), and a mixed-case option (C and D). The family is free for designers working directly with the museum.

name: Grand Stencil
classification_notes: A stencil cut of an elegant sans serif, placing it firmly in the display category; the stencil treatment and characterful letterforms distinguish it from utility grotesques.
summary: A single-style stencil sans with an extensive character set, fractions, icons and ornaments.
description: An elegant stencil sans serif offered in one style, with a character set that extends beyond the standard to include a bespoke range of fractions, icons and ornaments. The stencil treatment gives it a graphic, almost utilitarian quality while the letterforms retain refinement. Best suited to headlines, packaging and display work where a restrained stencil aesthetic is needed.
foundry_blurb: A2-Type describe Grand Stencil as an elegant, characterful sans serif stencil font available in one style, with an extensive character set plus a unique set of fractions, icons and ornaments.

name: Grot10
classification_notes: A contemporary revival based on Stephenson Blake's Victorian-era Grotesque No.10, placing it firmly in the British grotesque tradition with the characteristic single-storey forms and blunt terminals of that lineage.
summary: A contemporary reworking of Stephenson Blake's Sheffield-foundry Grotesque No.10, expanded across seven weights with condensed cuts originally commissioned for the RIBA Journal.
description: Drawn from the Stephenson Blake 'Grotesque No.10' as its principal source, this family carries the blunt, utilitarian character of the Victorian British grotesque tradition into a modern type system. Seven weights from Light to Black each have matching italics, while the Condensed subfamily, first cut for the RIBA Journal in 2013, brings tighter width options for magazine and editorial use. The family has since been extended with Semibold, Extrabold and Black to broaden its range for display and identity work.
foundry_blurb: A2-Type describe Grot10 as a contemporary take on the celebrated Grotesque series produced by the Stephenson Blake type foundry in Sheffield, with Grotesque No.10 as the principal source. The condensed cuts were originally designed for the Royal Institute of British Architects Members Magazine, Riba J, in 2013, and the family has since been expanded with additional weights.

name: Grot10 Condensed
classification_notes: Directly derived from Stephenson Blake's Grotesque No.10, a classic British grotesque series; the condensed cuts follow the same upright, workhorse grotesque tradition.
summary: A condensed mini-family drawn from A2-Type's revival of Stephenson Blake's Grotesque No.10, originally commissioned for the RIBA Journal in 2013.
description: Three weights of condensed grotesque — Light, Medium and Bold — derived from the Grot10 family, itself a contemporary reworking of Stephenson Blake's Sheffield-made Grotesque No.10. The cuts were first designed for the Royal Institute of British Architects' members magazine, Riba J, giving them a grounding in editorial use at tight column widths. The wider Grot10 family has since grown to include Semibold, Extrabold and Black, but the Condensed remains a compact standalone sub-family.
foundry_blurb: A2-Type describe Grot10 Condensed as a contemporary take on the celebrated Grotesque series by Stephenson Blake, with Grotesque No.10 as the principal source. The Condensed cuts were originally designed in 2013 for the RIBA Journal and form part of a wider Grot10 family that now spans Light through Black.

name: Grot12 Condensed
classification_notes: Inspired by Stephenson Blake's Victorian-era Grotesque series, Grot12 sits firmly in the British grotesque tradition rooted in the Industrial Revolution. The condensed width is one of three in an interconnected multi-width family.
summary: A condensed grotesque drawing on the Stephenson Blake Grotesque series, forming one third of A2-Type's three-width Grot12 family.
description: Grot12 Condensed is part of a wider family that spans three widths — Condensed, Normal and Extended — each sharing a common grotesque DNA anchored in the British Industrial Revolution. The Stephenson Blake Grotesque types serve as a reference point, lending the design its characteristic closed apertures and even stroke rhythm. Fourteen styles in the Condensed cut run from Thin to Black with matching italics throughout.
foundry_blurb: A2-Type describe Grot12 as anchored in the Industrial Revolution and partly inspired by the renowned Stephenson Blake series of Grotesque fonts. It is the follow-up to their Grot10 family released in 2010, offered in three widths — Extended, Normal and Condensed — across a range of weights from Thin to Black including italics, totalling 42 styles across the full family.

name: Grot12
classification_notes: Directly inspired by Stephenson Blake's Victorian-era Grotesque series, placing it firmly in the British grotesque tradition with industrial-era letterform references. Three widths and a full weight range confirm the grotesque sans genre.
summary: A 42-style grotesque family drawing on Stephenson Blake's Victorian industrial type, offered in three widths from Condensed to Extended.
description: Rooted in the Stephenson Blake Grotesque series of the Industrial Revolution era, this is A2-Type's follow-up to their earlier Grot10 family. The three-width structure — Condensed, Normal and Extended — runs from Thin to Black with matching italics across all cuts, giving 42 styles in total. A workmanlike British grotesque with historical grounding, suited to editorial and identity use where a period-inflected sans is needed.
foundry_blurb: A2-Type describe Grot12 as anchored in the Industrial Revolution and partly inspired by the renowned Stephenson Blake Grotesque fonts from that era. It is the follow-up to their Grot10 family from 2010, available in three widths — Extended, Normal and Condensed — with weights from Thin to Black including italics, totalling 42 styles.

name: Grot12 Normal
classification_notes: Directly inspired by the Stephenson Blake Grotesque series of the Industrial Revolution era, placing it firmly in the British grotesque tradition alongside its condensed and extended siblings.
summary: The standard-width cut of A2-Type's three-part grotesque family, drawn from the Stephenson Blake Grotesque series of the Industrial Revolution.
description: Part of a 42-style system spanning Condensed, Normal and Extended widths, this normal-width sub-family runs from Thin to Black with matching italics across seven weights. The reference point is the Stephenson Blake grotesque series, giving the design a characteristically British flavour rooted in nineteenth-century industrial print culture. It follows Grot10, A2-Type's earlier grotesque released in 2010, and functions well across editorial, identity and display contexts where historical British grotesque texture is wanted.
foundry_blurb: A2-Type describe Grot12 as anchored in the Industrial Revolution and partly inspired by the renowned Stephenson Blake Grotesque series. It is the follow-up to their Grot10 family from 2010, available in three widths — Condensed, Normal and Extended — across a total of 42 styles from Thin to Black including italics.

name: Impacto
classification_notes: Designed explicitly as a display face, Impacto draws from machine-cut wood type and historical sans and slab specimens, placing it firmly in the display category with wood type revival as its subcategory.
summary: A display family rooted in machine-cut wood type and historical sans and slab specimens, built for headline weight and poster-scale work.
description: Drawing on machine-cut wood type and nineteenth-century sans and slab specimens, Impacto runs eight weights from Thin to Black and extends into a Condensed subfamily for tighter column widths. The design is upright only, with no italics, reinforcing its identity as a face built for large-scale setting. The weight range is broader than most wood type revivals, making it usable across a wider set of display contexts.
foundry_blurb: A2-Type describe Impacto as designed specifically as a display font, inspired by machine-cut wood type and historical sans and slab specimens. They call it a solid, bold face created for high impact, noting it is a new font with plenty of history, and describe the Condensed styles as offering maximum typographic impact on a limited surface.

name: Impacto Condensed
classification_notes: Drawn for high-impact display use with reference to machine-cut wood type and historical sans and slab specimens; the condensed proportions and wide weight range place it firmly in the display category rather than a functional text sans.
summary: A condensed display family in eight weights, drawn from machine-cut wood type and historical sans and slab specimens for high-impact headline use.
description: Eight weights from Thin to Black, all in a condensed cut designed to deliver maximum typographic presence on a limited surface. The reference points are machine-cut wood type and nineteenth-century sans and slab faces from historical specimens, giving it a period flavour without being a revival. Well suited to posters, covers and editorial headlines where narrow measures demand a strong vertical axis.
foundry_blurb: A2-Type describe Impacto Condensed as a font designed specifically for display use, inspired by machine-cut wood type and historical sans and slab specimens. They call it a solid, bold face built for high impact — a new font with plenty of history — and note that the condensed styles offer maximum typographic impact on a limited surface.

name: Independent DOT
classification_notes: Independent DOT is a dot matrix interpretation of the Independent Sans grotesque, with letterforms constructed from discrete circular dots across three weights. Its display character places it firmly outside conventional text use.
summary: A dot matrix variant of Independent Sans, rendered across three weights using circular dot construction for display use.
description: Part of A2-Type's broader Independent family, DOT reinterprets the underlying grotesque letterforms as dot matrix constructions across Light, Medium and Black weights. The modular approach lends itself to display settings, signage and visual identities where a mechanical or retro-digital character is wanted. It sits alongside the stencil cuts in the Independent range as one of several decorative extensions from the core text and sans families.
foundry_blurb: A2-Type describe Independent DOT as an interpretation of the Independent Sans fonts, designed in three distinctive weights: Light, Medium and Black.

name: Independent Headline
classification_notes: A dedicated headline cut of the Independent serif family, designed for large display settings including the newspaper's masthead; shares structural proportions with the text cuts but is optimised for impact at size.
summary: A display serif cut designed for The Independent newspaper in 2013, used at the masthead and across large-scale print and digital headlines.
description: Designed as part of a wider system of interconnected typefaces for The Independent newspaper, this headline cut shares proportions and structure with the text and sans siblings but is drawn specifically for display use. The family earned a D&AD Yellow Pencil in 2014 and the Typeface Design prize at the Tokyo Type Directors Club in 2015. Available in Medium and Medium Italic, it suits editorial work where hierarchy and brand consistency across print and digital are both required.
foundry_blurb: A2-Type describe Independent Headline as part of a comprehensive suite of typefaces designed in 2013 for The Independent's daily print and digital editions. The fonts were conceived as interconnected typefaces sharing the same proportions and underlying structure. The headline cut was designed specifically for display setting, with a version used for the newspaper's masthead, and the wider family has since expanded to include stencil and slab variants.

name: Independent Sans
classification_notes: Designed for newspaper use with attention to legibility at text sizes and at display scale, sharing proportions with a broader serif and condensed family; the mix of optical warmth and functional range places it in the humanist sans tradition.
summary: A humanist sans designed in 2013 for The Independent newspaper, forming part of a unified type system covering serif, sans, condensed and stencil cuts.
description: Commissioned for The Independent's daily print and digital editions, Independent Sans shares its proportions and underlying structure with the broader Independent family, which includes a serif, condensed, slab and stencil. Five weights from Light to Black, each with an italic, give it sufficient range for both text and display use. The family received a D&AD Yellow Pencil in 2014 and the Typeface Design prize at the Tokyo TDC awards in 2015.
foundry_blurb: A2-Type describe Independent Sans as part of a suite of interconnected typefaces designed in 2013 for The Independent newspaper's print and digital editions, all sharing the same proportions and underlying structure.
